rallies.pat3: net shot → net shot → net shot
long service → short service → long service

notes.title: Lakshya Sen's drive was his biggest weapon
notes.body: 7 of his 16 outright winners came from the drive. Expect it — and train the counter.

notes.title: Lakshya Sen bled points on the defensive shot
notes.body: 6 points lost to defensive shot errors (2 into the net, 0 out). The single biggest fix available.

notes.title: Loh Kean Yew bled points on the lob
notes.body: 6 points lost to lob errors (0 into the net, 1 out). The single biggest fix available.

notes.title: Loh Kean Yew's errors were mostly unforced (62%)
notes.body: 15 of his 24 pressure-rated errors came from a comfortable position (<2.5 m/s required movement). These are free points to claw back — focus errors, not fitness.

notes.title: Serving was a liability for Loh Kean Yew
notes.body: He won 37% of his 30 service points (vs 54% when receiving).

notes.title: The “net shot → drive” exchange favored Lakshya Sen
notes.body: This ending sequence appeared 4 times and Lakshya Sen took 3 of them. Recognize the setup shot and break the pattern early.

commentary.headline: Lakshya Sen Triumphs in Error-Prone Final, Exploiting Loh Kean Yew's Defensive Lapses
commentary.match_story: Lakshya Sen clinched the YONEX SUNRISE India Open 2022 title over Loh Kean Yew, a match characterized by significant swings and a battle of offensive power against tactical consistency. The first set was a seesaw affair, featuring seven lead changes and a six-point run from Loh Kean Yew, before Sen edged it 24-22. Sen capitalized on Loh Kean Yew's higher error count, particularly when under pressure, to secure key points. In the second set, Sen maintained better control, limiting lead changes to just one and securing a 21-17 victory. While Loh Kean Yew displayed greater offensive firepower with 16 outright winners compared to Sen’s 10, his total of 35 errors, including 14 forced errors (more than double Sen's 6 forced errors), ultimately proved too costly. This indicates Sen's ability to capitalize on pressure situations, despite facing higher average movement demands himself (2.42 m/s faced pressure).
turning_points: Loh Kean Yew's high forced error count (14 vs. Sen's 6) indicates a breakdown under pressure from Sen's effective offensive play.
Loh Kean Yew bled 7 points on defensive shot errors, highlighting a critical vulnerability exploited by Sen.
Lakshya Sen's backhand accounted for 61% of his rally-ending errors despite being only 35% of his shots, signaling a tactical vulnerability for future opponents.
Loh Kean Yew's poor serving win rate of 42.5% significantly hampered his ability to dictate rallies from the outset.

players.name: Lakshya Sen
players.overview: Lakshya Sen secured the victory by maintaining slightly better consistency across all rally lengths and effectively capitalizing on his opponent's errors. His smash was a potent weapon, though he must address his own significant unforced error rate and backhand vulnerability.
strengths: Consistent performance across rally lengths, winning 54% of long (10+ shot), 52% of mid (5–9 shot), and 55% of short (≤4 shot) rallies.
Smash as a primary weapon, accounting for 8 of his 10 outright winners, demonstrating decisive attacking capability.
Effective receiving game, winning 23 out of 40 (57.5%) points when receiving serve, giving him an advantage in rally initiation.
Resilience under pressure, only committing 6 forced errors despite facing a higher average required speed of 2.42 m/s from Loh Kean Yew.
weaknesses: High unforced error rate, with 16 of his 22 pressure-rated errors (73%) being unforced, indicating lapses in concentration or shot selection from comfortable positions.
Vulnerable backhand, which constituted 35% of his shots but was responsible for 61% of his rally-ending errors (out of 23 total errors).
Lob errors, losing 6 points due to poor execution on lobs.
Applied less average pressure on his opponent (2.31 m/s) compared to the pressure he faced (2.42 m/s).
training_priorities: Focus on consistency drills for fundamental shots, specifically reducing unforced errors on lobs and general comfort-zone mistakes.
Intensive backhand technique and defensive consistency drills to improve reliability on that wing.
Decision-making drills to reduce unforced errors from comfortable positions, emphasizing shot selection and execution precision.
players.gameplan_against: Future opponents should consistently target Lakshya Sen's backhand, as it is disproportionately responsible for his errors (61% of his rally-ending errors). While he is resilient under physical pressure (only 6 forced errors), his high unforced error count (16 unforced vs. 6 forced) suggests that even without extreme physical duress, he can make mistakes. Apply varied pressure to elicit these unforced errors, and be prepared for his powerful smash (8 winners), denying him easy setups for this shot.

players.name: Loh Kean Yew
players.overview: Loh Kean Yew showed flashes of brilliant offense, leading in outright winners and clutch points, but his match was ultimately marred by an excessive number of errors, particularly from defensive positions and when serving. His inability to maintain consistency under pressure proved to be his undoing.
strengths: Superior offensive power, evidenced by 16 outright winners compared to Sen's 10, showcasing his attacking prowess.
Effective push/rush shots, accounting for 6 of his 16 winners, demonstrating strong net play and anticipation.
Mental toughness in tight situations, winning 10 out of 19 (52.6%) clutch points when the score was 18-all or higher.
Applied significant pressure, forcing his opponent to move at an average speed of 2.42 m/s.
weaknesses: High overall error count (35 errors), with a disproportionate number of forced errors (14 vs. Sen's 6), indicating susceptibility to effective pressure despite applying more pressure himself.
Major vulnerability in defensive shots, losing 7 points directly from errors on this shot type, the single biggest fix available.
Low service win rate, securing only 17 out of 40 (42.5%) points when serving, which is lower than his receiving win rate (50%).
Significant errors on drop shots (7 errors) and lobs (5 errors), indicating inconsistency in touch and defensive clears.
training_priorities: Intensive defensive shot drills, focusing on controlled clears and lifts from deep court, to reduce the 7 points lost to defensive shot errors.
Serve consistency and variation drills to improve his 42.5% service win rate.
Precision training for drop shots under pressure to minimize the 7 errors from this shot type.
players.gameplan_against: Opponents should aim to sustain pressure on Loh Kean Yew, especially targeting his defensive shots, as he bled 7 points through errors on these and accumulated 14 forced errors overall. While he applies high pressure (2.42 m/s applied speed), he is prone to breaking down defensively when effective pressure is applied. Exploit his weak serve by being aggressive on returns. Be wary of his push/rush shots (6 winners) and overall offensive capability (16 winners), but recognize that if kept under sustained pressure, his consistency will likely falter.
